help.admin_audit_detail: Every action is logged in a tamper-evident chain. View who answered calls, created notes, and made changes. The hash chain ensures logs cannot be modified after the fact.
help.admin_intro: As an admin, you can manage volunteers, shifts, and settings.
help.admin_reports_detail: Review incident reports filed by volunteers. View contact timelines showing all interactions with a caller. Caller identifiers are HMAC-hashed for privacy.
help.admin_shifts_detail: Set up recurring shift schedules with ring groups. Configure fallback groups for when no scheduled shift is active. Volunteers can only receive calls when clocked into their shift.
help.admin_spam_detail: Enable voice CAPTCHA to filter automated calls. Configure rate limiting per caller. Manage ban lists to block known bad actors. All settings can be adjusted in real-time.
help.admin_tip1: Create invites to add new volunteers
help.admin_tip2: Set up shift schedules for coverage
help.admin_tip3: Configure call routing and IVR settings
help.admin_tip4: Monitor the audit log for activity
help.admin_tip5: Use the ban list to block spam callers
help.admin_volunteers_detail: Invite new volunteers with invite codes. Assign roles, manage permissions, and deactivate accounts as needed. Volunteer personal information is only visible to admins.
help.faq_admin_a1: Go to Admin Panel > Invites and create a new invite code. Share the code securely with the volunteer. They'll use it during onboarding to register with your hub.
help.faq_admin_a2: Go to Admin Panel > Ban List and add the caller's phone number. Banned callers will hear a message and be disconnected automatically.
help.faq_admin_a3: Yes. The Audit Log in the Admin Panel shows all actions: calls answered, notes created, shifts started/ended, and more. The log is tamper-evident with hash chaining.
help.faq_admin_q1: How do I add a new volunteer?
help.faq_admin_q2: How do I ban a caller?
help.faq_admin_q3: Can I see all volunteer activity?
help.faq_break_a: Use the "Go On Break" button on the dashboard. You won't receive calls while on break.
help.faq_break_q: How do I take a break?
help.faq_calls_a1: Make sure you are clocked in for your shift and not on break. Check that your internet connection is stable (the relay connection indicator should be green).
help.faq_calls_a2: If you don't answer, the call continues ringing for other on-shift volunteers. The first volunteer to pick up handles the call. Missed calls are logged in the dashboard.
help.faq_calls_q1: Why am I not receiving calls?
help.faq_calls_q2: What happens if I miss a call?
help.faq_device_a: Go to Settings > Device Link and scan the QR code from your other device.
help.faq_device_q: How do I link another device?
help.faq_encrypt_a: All notes are encrypted end-to-end. Only you and admins can read them.
help.faq_export_a: Admins can export notes and reports from the admin panel.
help.faq_gs_a1: Ask your admin for an invite code. Enter the hub URL, create or import your identity, and set a PIN. You'll be ready to receive calls once you clock in for your shift.
help.faq_gs_a2: Your nsec is your secret key — like a password that proves your identity. It's generated on your device and never sent to the server. Back it up securely; if you lose it, you'll need a new identity.
help.faq_gs_a3: Yes. Use the Device Link feature in Settings to securely transfer your identity to another device using a QR code and encrypted key exchange.
help.faq_invite_a: Ask your admin for an invite code, then enter it on the login screen.
help.faq_key_a: Your identity key is generated locally and never sent to the server. Back it up securely.
help.faq_login_a: Enter your private key (nsec) or scan a device link QR code.
help.faq_notes_a1: Yes. Notes are end-to-end encrypted with a unique key per note. Only you and your admins can decrypt them. The server never sees the plaintext content.
help.faq_notes_a2: Notes are append-only for security. You can add additional notes but cannot modify existing ones. This ensures a reliable audit trail.
help.faq_notes_q2: Can I edit a note after saving?
help.faq_ring_a: All on-shift volunteers ring simultaneously. The first to pick up gets the call.
help.faq_shifts_a: Check the Shifts tab for your upcoming shifts and sign up for available ones.
help.faq_shifts_q: Where do I see my shifts?
help.faq_spam_a: Admins can configure ban lists, voice CAPTCHA, and rate limiting in settings.
help.sec_auth_detail: Login uses cryptographic keypairs (Nostr). No passwords stored on servers.
help.sec_notes_detail: Each note is encrypted with a unique key, wrapped for each authorized reader.
help.sec_reports_detail: Reports are encrypted and only accessible to admins.
help.sec_sessions_detail: Sessions are bound to your device via WebAuthn. Stolen tokens cannot be used elsewhere.
help.subtitle: How to use the app
help.title: Help
help.vol_calls_detail: When a caller dials the hotline, all on-shift volunteers ring simultaneously. The first volunteer to pick up handles the call — all other rings are cancelled. Calls are routed based on your shift schedule and availability.
help.vol_encryption_detail: Your secret key (nsec) never leaves your device. Notes and messages are encrypted locally before being sent to the server. Even if the server is compromised, your data remains private.
help.vol_notes_detail: Create notes during or after calls to document interactions. Notes support custom fields defined by your admin. All note content is end-to-end encrypted — only you and admins can read them.
help.vol_safety_detail: Lock the app when stepping away. Use PIN protection to prevent unauthorized access. If you're in a dangerous situation, use Emergency Wipe in Settings to permanently delete all app data from this device.
help.vol_shift_detail: Clock in when your shift starts to begin receiving calls. Use break mode when you need a pause — you'll stop receiving calls but stay logged in. Clock out at the end of your shift.
help.volunteer_intro: Welcome! Here's how to get started as a volunteer.
help.volunteer_tip1: Clock in at the start of your shift
help.volunteer_tip2: Answer calls promptly when they ring
help.volunteer_tip3: Take notes during or after each call
help.volunteer_tip4: Use the break button if you need a moment
help.volunteer_tip5: Clock out when your shift ends
help.adminIntro: As an admin, you manage volunteers, shifts, call settings, and security features.
help.adminTip1: Invite volunteers or reporters from the Volunteers page — choose their role when creating the invite
help.adminTip2: Set up shifts with time ranges and assigned volunteers for automatic call routing
help.adminTip3: Configure telephony, transcription, CAPTCHA, and rate limiting in Admin Settings
help.adminTip4: Monitor volunteer status and active calls from your dashboard
help.adminTip5: Review the Audit Log for a history of all system events
help.faqBreakA: When on break, incoming calls are not routed to you. Other on-shift volunteers will still receive them. Use the break toggle on your dashboard or press Ctrl+Shift+B.
help.faqEncryptA: Yes. Notes are encrypted end-to-end using your keypair before leaving your device. Only you and authorized admins can decrypt them. The server never sees plaintext.
help.faqExportA: Yes. Go to Notes and use the Export button. Notes are exported encrypted — you'll need your secret key to decrypt them.
help.faqInviteA: Go to Volunteers and click "Invite Volunteer". Choose the role (Volunteer, Admin, or Reporter), fill in their name and phone, and share the invite link. Each invite code is single-use and expires in 7 days.
help.faqKeyA: Your key is stored encrypted on this device, protected by your PIN. During onboarding you received a recovery key — use it to restore access on a new device. You can also link devices via QR code from Settings. Contact your admin if you've lost both your PIN and recovery key.
help.faqKeyQ: What is my secret key and where do I find it?
help.faqLoginA: Enter your 6-digit PIN on the login page to unlock your key store. If you have a passkey set up, you can use that instead. To set up a new device, use the "Link this device" option to scan a QR code from your primary device.
help.faqProviderA: Go to Admin Settings > Telephony Provider. Select your provider, enter credentials, and test the connection before saving. Supported providers: Twilio, SignalWire, Vonage, Plivo, Asterisk.
help.faqProviderQ: How do I change the telephony provider?
help.faqPwaA: Yes. In your browser, look for 'Install' or 'Add to Home Screen' in the menu. The app will install as 'Hotline' for security — it doesn't reveal the organization name.
help.faqPwaQ: Can I install this as an app?
help.faqRingA: Check that: (1) you're on-shift (see your shift schedule), (2) you're not on break, (3) you're not already on a call, and (4) your browser/phone notifications are enabled.
help.faqRingQ: Why am I not receiving calls?
help.faqShiftsA: Create shifts in the Shifts page with a name, time range, and days of the week. Assign volunteers to each shift. When a shift is active, assigned volunteers receive incoming calls via parallel ringing.
help.faqSpamA: Three options: (1) Ban specific numbers in the Ban List, (2) Enable Voice CAPTCHA in Admin Settings to require callers to enter digits, (3) Enable Rate Limiting to block repeated calls from the same number.
help.faqSpamQ: How do I deal with spam calls?
help.faqVoicemailA: If no volunteer answers within the queue timeout, the caller is sent to voicemail (if enabled). Admins can configure the timeout and voicemail settings.
help.faqVoicemailQ: What happens if no one answers?
help.quickNavDesc: Press Ctrl+K to open the command palette for quick navigation and search.
help.reporterIntro: As a reporter, you can submit encrypted reports and communicate with administrators through a secure message thread.
help.reporterTip1: Click "New" to create a report with a title, category, and details
help.reporterTip2: Your report details are encrypted end-to-end — only you and admins can read them
help.reporterTip3: Reply to your own reports to provide updates or additional information
help.reporterTip4: Attach files to messages — they are encrypted before upload
help.volunteerIntro: As a volunteer, you answer incoming calls during your assigned shifts and take encrypted notes.
help.volunteerTip1: Your dashboard shows shift status, active calls, and incoming calls
help.volunteerTip2: When a call rings, click "Answer" — all on-shift volunteers ring simultaneously, first to answer gets the call
help.volunteerTip3: Take notes during calls — they're encrypted before leaving your device
help.volunteerTip4: Use 'Take a Break' when you need to step away — you won't receive calls
help.volunteerTip5: Report spam calls or ban numbers directly from the active call panel

telephonyProvider.phoneNumberHelp: The phone number callers dial (E.164 format)

providerDescriptions.twilio: Industry-standard cloud telephony. Easiest to set up with excellent documentation.
providerDescriptions.signalwire: Twilio-compatible API at lower cost. Drop-in replacement for most use cases.
providerDescriptions.vonage: Global telephony provider with strong international coverage and competitive pricing.
providerDescriptions.plivo: Cost-effective cloud telephony with a clean API. Good for budget-conscious deployments.
providerDescriptions.asterisk: Self-hosted PBX for maximum control and lowest per-minute cost. Requires server setup.

telephonyProvider.title: Telephony Provider
telephonyProvider.twimlAppSidHelp: 음성 URL이 웹훅을 가리키는 TwiML App을 생성하세요
telephonyProvider.webrtcConfigHelp: 자원봉사자를 위한 브라우저 기반 통화를 활성화합니다. 추가 자격 증명이 필요합니다.

transcription.allowOptOutDescription: 비활성화 시 자원봉사자는 통화 전사를 거부할 수 없습니다
transcription.clientSideDescription: Transcribe calls locally in your browser. Audio never leaves your device.
transcription.localMicOnlyDescription: Only your microphone audio is captured. The caller's voice is not accessible for transcription.
transcription.notSupported: Your browser does not support in-browser transcription
transcription.title: 전사

webauthn.description: 비밀 키 없이 기기 생체 인식 또는 PIN으로 로그인하세요.

webauthn.policyDescription: 사용자 그룹에 패스키를 요구합니다. 패스키가 없는 사용자는 설정하라는 메시지가 표시됩니다.
